sentences.sentence: In timed trials, contestants were challenged to look at and then recite a two-page poem, memorize rows of 40-digit numbers, recall the names of 110 people after looking at their photographs, and perform seven other feats of extraordinary retention.
sentences.translation: 在限时比赛中，参赛者要挑战阅读并背诵一首两页长的诗、记住一行行40位数字、在看过照片后说出110个人的姓名，并完成另外七项非凡的记忆壮举。

structure.text: to look at and then recite a two-page poem
recall the names of 110 people after looking at their photographs
and perform seven other feats of extraordinary retention

difficult_points: were challenged to 后接四个并列的不定式，共同作宾语补足语。
after looking at their photographs 表示看过照片之后，说明回忆姓名的先后顺序。
40-digit 和 110 people 是复合数词修饰语，直接作前置定语。

sentences.sentence: Some tests took just a few minutes; others lasted hours.
sentences.translation: 有些测试只需几分钟，另一些则耗时数小时。
difficult_points: 分号连接两个并列的独立分句，无需连词。
just a few minutes 与 hours 形成时间长短的对比。

sentences.sentence: In the 14 years since the World Memory Championships was founded, no one has memorized the order of a shuffled deck of playing cards in less than 30 seconds.
sentences.translation: 自世界记忆锦标赛创办以来的14年里，还没有人能在30秒之内记住一副洗过的扑克牌的顺序。

structure.text: since the World Memory Championships was founded
has memorized the order of a shuffled deck of playing cards

difficult_points: since 引导的时间状语从句修饰前面的 14 years，说明这段时间的起点。
has memorized 是现在完成时，强调从创办至今从未有人做到。
in less than 30 seconds 限定完成记忆的时间上限。

expressions.text: a shuffled deck of playing cards

sentences.sentence: That nice round number has become the four-minute mile of competitive memory, a benchmark that the world's best "mental athletes," as some of them like to be called, is closing in on.
sentences.translation: 这个漂亮的整数已经成为竞技记忆界的“四分钟一英里”——一个世界顶尖“脑力运动员”（有些人喜欢这样称呼自己）正在逐渐逼近的基准。

structure.text: the four-minute mile of competitive memory
that the world's best "mental athletes," as some of them like to be called, is closing in on
as some of them like to be called

difficult_points: the four-minute mile 借用田径界的著名说法，比喻竞技记忆界普遍难以打破的门槛。
that 引导定语从句修饰 benchmark，从句谓语 is closing in on 的宾语正是先行词 benchmark。
as some of them like to be called 是插入的方式状语从句，说明“脑力运动员”是他们自称的叫法。

sentences.sentence: Most contestants claim to have just average memories, and scientific testing confirms that they're not just being modest.
sentences.translation: 大多数参赛者都声称自己的记忆力只是普通水平，而科学测试证实，他们这么说并非只是谦虚。

structure.text: claim to have just average memories
that they're not just being modest

difficult_points: claim to have 表示声称自己拥有，不定式作 claim 的宾语。
that 引导宾语从句，作 confirms 的宾语。
not just being modest 意为并非仅仅是谦虚。

sentences.sentence: Their feats are based on tricks that capitalize on how the human brain encodes information.
sentences.translation: 他们的壮举依靠的是一些技巧，这些技巧利用了人脑编码信息的方式。

structure.text: that capitalize on how the human brain encodes information
how the human brain encodes information

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句修饰 tricks。
capitalize on 意为利用，其宾语是 how 引导的名词从句。
how 从句说明人脑编码信息的具体方式。

sentences.sentence: Anyone can learn them.
sentences.translation: 任何人都能学会这些技巧。
difficult_points: them 指代上一句提到的 tricks（技巧）。
简短独句强调这些记忆技巧并非天赋，人人可学。

sentences.sentence: Psychologists Elizabeth Valentine and John Wilding, authors of the monograph Superior Memory, recently teamed up with Eleanor Maguire, a neuroscientist at University College London to study eight people, including Karsten, who had finished near the top of the World Memory Championships.
sentences.translation: 心理学家伊丽莎白·瓦伦丁和约翰·怀尔丁——专著《超凡记忆》的作者——最近与伦敦大学学院的神经科学家埃莉诺·马奎尔合作，研究了包括卡斯滕在内的八个人，这些人都曾在世界记忆锦标赛中名列前茅。

structure.text: Psychologists Elizabeth Valentine and John Wilding
authors of the monograph Superior Memory
recently teamed up with Eleanor Maguire
a neuroscientist at University College London
to study eight people, including Karsten
who had finished near the top of the World Memory Championships

difficult_points: authors of the monograph Superior Memory 是插入的同位语，说明两位心理学家的身份。
a neuroscientist at University College London 是同位语，补充说明 Eleanor Maguire 的身份。
who 引导的定语从句修饰 eight people，指这些人曾在比赛中名列前茅。

sentences.sentence: They wondered if the contestants' brains were different in some way.
sentences.translation: 他们想知道这些参赛者的大脑是否在某些方面有所不同。

structure.text: if the contestants' brains were different in some way

difficult_points: if 引导宾语从句，相当于 whether，表示是否。
in some way 修饰 different，表示在某种程度/方面。

sentences.sentence: The researchers put the competitors and a group of control subjects into an MRI machine and asked them to perform several different memory tests while their brains were being scanned.
sentences.translation: 研究人员把参赛选手和一组对照组受试者一起放进核磁共振成像仪，并要求他们在大脑接受扫描的同时完成几项不同的记忆测试。

structure.text: put the competitors and a group of control subjects into an MRI machine
and asked them to perform several different memory tests
to perform several different memory tests
while their brains were being scanned

difficult_points: put...into 与 asked...to 是并列的两个谓语，共用主语 the researchers。
while 引导时间状语从句，were being scanned 是过去进行时的被动语态。

sentences.sentence: When it came to memorizing sequences of three-digit numbers, the difference between the memory contestant and the control subjects was, as expected, immense.
sentences.translation: 说到记忆一串串三位数的数字，记忆选手和对照组受试者之间的差距，正如预期的那样，十分悬殊。

structure.text: When it came to memorizing sequences of three-digit numbers
the difference between the memory contestant and the control subjects

difficult_points: when it comes to doing 是固定表达，意为说到/涉及到……。
as expected 是插入的比较状语从句，省略了主谓，意为正如预期的那样。
was 与表语 immense 之间插入了 as expected，阅读时需跳过插入语理解主干。

sentences.sentence: However, when they were shown photographs of magnified snowflakes, images that the competitors had never tried to memorize before, the champions did no better than the control group.
sentences.translation: 然而，当他们被给看放大的雪花照片——这是参赛者以前从未尝试记忆过的图像——时，冠军选手的表现并不比对照组好。

structure.text: when they were shown photographs of magnified snowflakes
images that the competitors had never tried to memorize before
that the competitors had never tried to memorize before
did no better than the control group

difficult_points: However 是连接副词，标志转折但不引导从句。
images that... 是对 photographs of magnified snowflakes 的同位语说明。
did no better than 表示并不比……更好，强调冠军和对照组表现相当。

sentences.sentence: When the researchers analyzed the brain scans, they found that the memory champs were activating some brain regions that were different from those the control subjects were using.
sentences.translation: 当研究人员分析这些脑部扫描图像时，他们发现记忆冠军激活的某些脑区，与对照组受试者所使用的脑区并不相同。

structure.text: When the researchers analyzed the brain scans
that the memory champs were activating some brain regions
that were different from those the control subjects were using
those the control subjects were using

difficult_points: that 引导宾语从句，作 found 的宾语。
that were different from... 是定语从句修饰 brain regions。
those 后省略了关系代词 that/which，指代 the control subjects were using 的脑区。

sentences.sentence: These regions, which included the right posterior hippocampus, are known to be involved in visual memory and spatial navigation.
sentences.translation: 这些脑区——包括右侧海马体后部——已知与视觉记忆和空间导航有关。

structure.text: which included the right posterior hippocampus
to be involved in visual memory and spatial navigation

difficult_points: which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明这些脑区包括右侧海马体后部。
are known to be involved in 是 be known to do 结构，表示已知是/被认为是。

sentences.sentence: It might seem odd that the memory contestants would use visual imagery and spatial navigation to remember numbers, but the activity makes sense when their techniques are revealed.
sentences.translation: 记忆选手竟然会用视觉形象和空间导航来记数字，这可能显得奇怪，但当他们的技巧被揭示后，这种做法就说得通了。

structure.text: that the memory contestants would use visual imagery and spatial navigation to remember numbers

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，真正的主语是 that 引导的从句。
to remember numbers 是不定式作目的状语。
when their techniques are revealed 是被动语态的时间状语从句。

sentences.sentence: Cooke, a 23-year-old cognitive-science graduate student with a shoulder-length mop of curly hair, is a grand master of brain storage.
sentences.translation: 库克是一名23岁的认知科学研究生，留着一头齐肩的卷发，是记忆存储领域的宗师级人物。

structure.text: a 23-year-old cognitive-science graduate student with a shoulder-length mop of curly hair
a grand master of brain storage

difficult_points: 插入的同位语描述 Cooke 的身份与外貌，起补充说明作用。
a shoulder-length mop of curly hair 用 mop（拖把）比喻蓬乱的卷发，形象化描述发型。

sentences.sentence: He can memorize the order of 10 decks of playing cards in less than an hour or one deck of cards in less than a minute.
sentences.translation: 他能在不到一小时内记住10副扑克牌的顺序，或者在不到一分钟内记住一副牌。

structure.text: can memorize the order of 10 decks of playing cards
or one deck of cards in less than a minute

difficult_points: or 并列连接两种情况，省略了重复的谓语 can memorize (the order of)。
in less than 表示在不到……的时间内，两处并列使用体现对比。

sentences.sentence: He is closing in on the 30-second deck.
sentences.translation: 他正逐渐逼近30秒记住一副牌的目标。
difficult_points: the 30-second deck 是名词化表达，指能在30秒内记住的一副牌这一目标。
closing in on 表示正在逐步接近某目标。

sentences.sentence: In the Lamb and Flag, Cooke pulled out a deck of cards and shuffled it.
sentences.translation: 在“羊羔与旗帜”酒吧里，库克拿出一副牌洗了洗。

structure.text: pulled out a deck of cards

difficult_points: the Lamb and Flag 是一家酒吧的专有名称，非普通名词短语。
pulled out 与 shuffled 是并列谓语，共享主语 Cooke。

sentences.sentence: He held up three cards – the 7 of spades, the queen of clubs, and the 10 of spades.
sentences.translation: 他举起三张牌——黑桃7、梅花皇后和黑桃10。

structure.text: the 7 of spades, the queen of clubs, and the 10 of spades

difficult_points: 破折号后列举的三张牌具体说明前面的 three cards。
the queen of clubs 中 queen 指扑克牌里的皇后（Q），而非真实的女王。

sentences.sentence: He pointed at a fireplace and said, "Destiny's Child is whacking Franz Schubert with handbags."
sentences.translation: 他指着一处壁炉说：“‘真命天女’正在用手提包痛打弗朗茨·舒伯特。”

structure.text: "Destiny's Child is whacking Franz Schubert with handbags."

difficult_points: Destiny's Child 和 Franz Schubert 分别指库克为这两张牌关联的人物形象（人物、动作、物体记忆法）。
whacking...with handbags 描述记忆联想中的具体动作画面，需结合上下文理解这是记忆技巧而非字面事件。

sentences.sentence: The next three cards were the king of hearts, the king of spades, and the jack of clubs.
sentences.translation: 接下来的三张牌是红桃K、黑桃K和梅花J。

structure.text: the king of hearts, the king of spades, and the jack of clubs

difficult_points: 并列的三个表语具体说明接下来抽到的三张牌。
king 和 jack 分别指扑克牌中的国王（K）和杰克/J牌，延续上一句的记牌情境。

sentences.sentence: How did he do it?
sentences.translation: 他是怎么做到的？
difficult_points: 一般疑问句的倒装结构，did 提前，引出下文对方法的说明。
it 指代前文库克记忆扑克牌顺序这件事。

sentences.sentence: Cooke has already memorized a specific person, verb, and object that he associates with each card in the deck.
sentences.translation: 库克已经为牌堆中的每一张牌都记好了一个特定的人物、动作和物体，将它们联系在一起。

structure.text: has already memorized a specific person, verb, and object
that he associates with each card in the deck

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句，修饰并列的 person, verb, and object 三者。
verb 和 object 在此并非语法术语，而是库克记忆法中动作和物体的代称。

sentences.sentence: For example, for the 7 of spades, the person (or, in this case, persons) is always the singing group Destiny's Child, the action is surviving a storm, and the image is a dinghy.
sentences.translation: 举例来说，对于黑桃7，这个“人物”（在这种情况下是“人物们”）一直是歌唱组合“真命天女”，“动作”是在风暴中幸存，“物体”是一艘小艇。

structure.text: For example, for the 7 of spades
is always the singing group Destiny's Child
the action is surviving a storm
and the image is a dinghy

difficult_points: (or, in this case, persons) 是插入语，说明因为是组合而非个人，用复数更准确。
三个并列分句分别说明人物、动作、物体三要素在这张牌上的具体内容。

sentences.sentence: The queen of clubs is always his friend Henrietta, the action is thwacking with a handbag, and the image is of wardrobes filled with designer clothes.
sentences.translation: 梅花皇后一直是他的朋友亨丽埃塔，“动作”是用手提包痛打，“物体”则是装满名牌服装的衣柜。

structure.text: the action is thwacking with a handbag
and the image is of wardrobes filled with designer clothes

difficult_points: is of wardrobes 相当于 is wardrobes，of 在此引出表语的具体内容。
filled with designer clothes 是过去分词短语，修饰 wardrobes。

sentences.sentence: When Cooke commits a deck to memory, he does it three cards at a time.
sentences.translation: 库克在记住一副牌时，是每次三张三张地记的。

structure.text: When Cooke commits a deck to memory
does it three cards at a time

difficult_points: commit...to memory 是固定搭配，意为把……记住。
three cards at a time 作方式状语，说明每次处理的数量单位。

sentences.sentence: Every three-card group forms a single image of a person doing something to an object.
sentences.translation: 每三张一组的牌都会构成一个单一的画面：某个人对某件物体做某个动作。

structure.text: a single image of a person doing something to an object

difficult_points: doing something to an object 是现在分词短语，修饰 a person，说明这个人正在对物体施加的动作。
forms 呼应上一句的三要素设定，说明每三张牌组合成一个完整画面。

sentences.sentence: The first card in the triplet becomes the person, the second the verb, the third the object.
sentences.translation: 三张一组中的第一张牌代表人物，第二张代表动作，第三张代表物体。

structure.text: The first card in the triplet

difficult_points: 后两个并列分句省略了重复的谓语 becomes，即 the second (becomes) the verb。
the triplet 指前文提到的三张一组的牌，first/second/third 依次对应人物、动作、物体。

sentences.sentence: He then places those images along a specific familiar route, such as the one he took through the Lamb and Flag.
sentences.translation: 然后他把这些画面沿着一条特定的熟悉路线摆放，比如他在“羊羔与旗帜”酒吧里走过的那条路线。

structure.text: then places those images along a specific familiar route
such as the one he took through the Lamb and Flag
he took through the Lamb and Flag

difficult_points: such as 引出具体的例子，说明熟悉路线指的是什么。
the one 指代 route，后面省略了关系代词 that/which。

sentences.sentence: In competitions, he uses an imaginary route that he has designed to be as smooth and downhill as possible.
sentences.translation: 在比赛中，他使用的是一条想象出来的路线，这条路线被他设计得尽可能平坦顺畅、一路下坡。

structure.text: that he has designed to be as smooth and downhill as possible
to be as smooth and downhill as possible

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句修饰 route。
as smooth and downhill as possible 是原级比较结构，表示尽可能……。

sentences.sentence: When it comes time to recall, Cooke takes a mental walk along his route and translates the images into cards.That's why the MRIs of the memory contestants showed activation in the brain areas associated with visual imagery and spatial navigation.
sentences.translation: 到了需要回忆的时候，库克会沿着自己的路线做一次“心理漫步”，把画面重新转译成扑克牌。这就是为什么记忆选手的核磁共振扫描显示，与视觉形象和空间导航相关的脑区出现了激活。

structure.text: When it comes time to recall
takes a mental walk along his route
and translates the images into cards
That's why the MRIs of the memory contestants showed activation in the brain areas
associated with visual imagery and spatial navigation

difficult_points: 源文本句在 cards. 与 That's 之间缺少空格，是原始素材的排版缺失，按原文逐字保留，不做增补。
that's why 引导原因结果，指代前文库克的记忆方法，说明为何脑扫描会显示特定区域激活。
associated with visual imagery and spatial navigation 是过去分词短语，修饰 brain areas。

sentences.sentence: The more resonant the images are, the more difficult they are to forget.
sentences.translation: 画面越是引发共鸣，就越难以忘记。

structure.text: The more resonant the images are
the more difficult they are to forget

difficult_points: the more...the more... 是越……越……的比较级平行结构。
to forget 是不定式，修饰 difficult，说明难以忘记这一方面。

expressions.meaning_zh: 引发共鸣的；有感染力的

sentences.sentence: But even meaningful information is hard to remember when there's a lot of it.
sentences.translation: 但即使是有意义的信息，一旦数量庞大，也很难记住。

structure.text: when there's a lot of it

difficult_points: even 强调即使是有意义的信息也不例外。
when there's a lot of it 说明信息量大是难以记住的条件。

sentences.sentence: That's why competitive memorizers place their images along an imaginary route.
sentences.translation: 这就是为什么参加记忆比赛的人会把自己的画面沿着一条想象中的路线摆放。

structure.text: competitive memorizers place their images along an imaginary route

difficult_points: that's why 引导表语从句，指代前面提到的信息量大难以记忆这一事实。
why 后接的完整分句（competitive memorizers place...）说明记忆者采用这一方法的原因结果关系。

sentences.sentence: That technique, known as the loci method, reportedly originated in 477 B.C. with the Greek poet Simonides of Ceos.
sentences.translation: 据说，这种被称为“位置记忆法”的技巧起源于公元前477年，创始人是希腊诗人凯奥斯的西蒙尼德斯。

structure.text: with the Greek poet Simonides of Ceos

difficult_points: known as the loci method 是过去分词短语，修饰 technique，补充说明其名称。
reportedly 是句子副词，表示据说，说明起源的说法并非绝对确定。
with 引出该技巧的创始/关联人物。

sentences.sentence: Simonides was the sole survivor of a roof collapse that killed all the other guests at a royal banquet.
sentences.translation: 西蒙尼德斯是一场屋顶坍塌事故中唯一的幸存者，那场事故夺去了皇室宴会上所有其他宾客的性命。

structure.text: the sole survivor of a roof collapse
that killed all the other guests at a royal banquet

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句修饰 a roof collapse，说明这场坍塌事故的后果。
the sole survivor 强调西蒙尼德斯是唯一幸存者，为下文他凭记忆复原名单做铺垫。

sentences.sentence: The bodies were mangled beyond recognition, but Simonides was able to reconstruct the guest list by closing his eyes and recalling each individual around the dinner table.
sentences.translation: 遗体都被毁损得难以辨认，但西蒙尼德斯却能够通过闭上双眼、回忆餐桌旁每个人的位置，重新列出完整的宾客名单。

structure.text: The bodies were mangled beyond recognition
but Simonides was able to reconstruct the guest list
by closing his eyes and recalling each individual around the dinner table

difficult_points: beyond recognition 表示程度之深以至于无法辨认。
by doing 引出西蒙尼德斯重建名单所采用的方式，closing 和 recalling 是并列的动名词。

sentences.sentence: What he had discovered was that our brains are exceptionally good at remembering images and spatial information.
sentences.translation: 他发现的是：人类大脑格外擅长记忆图像和空间信息。

structure.text: that our brains are exceptionally good at remembering images and spatial information

difficult_points: what 引导主语从句，指代西蒙尼德斯所发现的内容。
that 引导表语从句，说明发现的具体内容。
be good at 后接动名词，表示擅长于。

sentences.sentence: Evolutionary psychologists have offered an explanation: Presumably, our ancestors found it important to recall where they found their last meal or the way back to the cave.
sentences.translation: 进化心理学家给出了一种解释：据推测，我们的祖先认为记住上一顿食物的来源，或者回洞穴的路，是很重要的事。

structure.text: Presumably, our ancestors found it important
to recall where they found their last meal or the way back to the cave
where they found their last meal

difficult_points: 冒号后的内容具体说明 an explanation 的内容。
found it important to recall... 中 it 是形式宾语，真正的宾语是后面的不定式。
where 引导的从句作 recall 的宾语之一，与 the way back to the cave 并列。

sentences.sentence: After Simonides' discovery, the loci method became popular across ancient Greece as a trick for memorizing speeches and texts.
sentences.translation: 在西蒙尼德斯的这一发现之后，位置记忆法作为一种记忆演讲稿和文本的技巧，在整个古希腊流行开来。

structure.text: as a trick for memorizing speeches and texts

difficult_points: as a trick for doing 说明该方法被当作什么用途的技巧使用。
After 引导的时间状语说明位置记忆法流行是在西蒙尼德斯发现之后。

sentences.sentence: Aristotle wrote about it, and later a number of treatises on the art of memory were published in Rome.
sentences.translation: 亚里士多德曾写文章论及此事，后来罗马又出版了不少关于记忆艺术的论著。

structure.text: and later a number of treatises on the art of memory were published in Rome

difficult_points: it 指代前面提到的 loci method（位置记忆法）。
were published 是被动语态，强调论著被出版这一事实而非作者。

sentences.sentence: Before printed books, the art of memory was considered a staple of classical education, on a par with grammar, logic, and rhetoric.
sentences.translation: 在印刷书籍出现之前，记忆术被视为古典教育的重要组成部分，与语法、逻辑和修辞相提并论。

structure.text: was considered a staple of classical education
on a par with grammar, logic, and rhetoric

difficult_points: was considered 是被动结构，后接名词作宾语补足语 a staple of classical education。
on a par with 表示与……相当/并列。

sentences.sentence: The most famous of the naturals was the Russian journalist S.V. Shereshevski, who could recall long lists of numbers memorized decades earlier, as well as poems, strings of nonsense syllables, and just about anything else he was asked to remember.
sentences.translation: 这些天生记忆高手中最有名的，是俄罗斯记者S.V.舍雷舍夫斯基，他能回忆起几十年前记住的一长串数字，还有诗歌、一串串无意义的音节，以及几乎任何被要求记住的东西。

structure.text: The most famous of the naturals
who could recall long lists of numbers memorized decades earlier, as well as poems, strings of nonsense syllables, and just about anything else he was asked to remember

difficult_points: the naturals 指天生的记忆高手，the + 形容词表示一类人。
memorized decades earlier 是过去分词短语，修饰 numbers，说明这些数字是几十年前记住的。
anything else 后省略了关系代词 that，he was asked to remember 是省略引导词的定语从句。

sentences.sentence: "The capacity of his memory had no distinct limits," wrote Alexander Luria, the Russian psychologist who studies Shereshevski also had synesthesia, a rare condition in which the senses become intertwined.
sentences.translation: “他的记忆容量没有明确的界限，”研究他的俄罗斯心理学家亚历山大·卢里亚写道——舍雷舍夫斯基还患有联觉，这是一种感官相互交织的罕见症状。

structure.text: "The capacity of his memory had no distinct limits,"
a rare condition in which the senses become intertwined
in which the senses become intertwined

difficult_points: 引语后 wrote Alexander Luria 是引述动词提前的倒装结构，主语置于动词之后。
源文在 studies 与 Shereshevski 之间缺少标点和文字（疑似排版/换行造成的缺失），推测原意应为研究舍雷舍夫斯基多年后另起一句，此处按原文原样保留，不做增补。
in which the senses become intertwined 是介词+which 引导的定语从句，修饰 a rare condition。

sentences.sentence: For example, every number may be associated with a color or every word with a taste.
sentences.translation: 比如，每个数字可能都会与某种颜色相关联，每个词则可能与某种味道相关联。

structure.text: every number may be associated with a color
or every word with a taste

difficult_points: or 后面的分句省略了重复的谓语 may be associated。
本句用数字和词语两个例子具体说明上一句提到的联觉现象。

sentences.sentence: Synesthetic reactions evoke a response in more areas of the brain, making memory easier.
sentences.translation: 联觉反应会在大脑更多区域引发反应，从而让记忆变得更容易。

structure.text: evoke a response in more areas of the brain

difficult_points: making memory easier 是现在分词短语作结果状语，说明前面动作带来的结果。
evoke a response in more areas 呼应前文舍雷舍夫斯基联觉带来记忆增强的说法。

sentences.sentence: K. Anders Ericsson, a Swedish-born psychologist at Florida State University, thinks anyone can acquire Shereshevski's skills.
sentences.translation: K·安德斯·埃里克森是佛罗里达州立大学一位瑞典裔心理学家，他认为任何人都可以掌握舍雷舍夫斯基那样的技能。

structure.text: a Swedish-born psychologist at Florida State University

difficult_points: 插入的同位语说明埃里克森的身份和所属大学。
thinks 后省略了 that，直接接宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: He cites an experiment with S. F., an undergraduate who was paid to take a standard test of memory called the digit span for one hour a day, two or three days a week.
sentences.translation: 他引用了一个针对S.F.的实验，S.F.是一名本科生，受雇每周两三天、每天一小时接受一种叫作“数字广度”的标准记忆测试。

structure.text: cites an experiment with S. F.
who was paid to take a standard test of memory called the digit span
for one hour a day, two or three days a week

difficult_points: S. F. 是化名缩写，指代该实验中的受试者。
called the digit span 是过去分词短语，修饰 test，说明测试的名称。
for one hour a day, two or three days a week 说明测试的频率与时长。

sentences.sentence: When he started, he could hold, like most people, only about seven digits in his head at any given time (conveniently, the length of a phone number).
sentences.translation: 他刚开始时，和大多数人一样，脑海中同一时间只能记住大约七位数字（恰好是一个电话号码的长度）。

structure.text: only about seven digits in his head at any given time
conveniently, the length of a phone number

difficult_points: like most people 是插入的比较状语，说明这一记忆容量并非个例。
括号内容是补充说明，指出七位数恰好等于电话号码长度。

sentences.sentence: Over two years, S. F. completed 250 hours of testing.
sentences.translation: 在两年时间里，S.F.完成了250个小时的测试。
difficult_points: over 表示在……期间/历时，强调持续的时间跨度。
250 hours of testing 说明测试累计的总时长。

sentences.sentence: By then, he had stretched his digit span from 7 to more than 80.
sentences.translation: 到那时，他已经把自己的数字广度从7位提升到了80多位。

structure.text: had stretched his digit span from 7 to more than 80

difficult_points: had stretched 是过去完成时，强调到那个时间点之前已经完成的变化。
from...to... 说明数字广度提升的起止范围。

sentences.sentence: The study of S. F. led Ericsson to believe that innately superior memory doesn't exist at all.
sentences.translation: 对S.F.的这项研究让埃里克森相信，天生优越的记忆力根本不存在。

structure.text: that innately superior memory doesn't exist at all

difficult_points: lead somebody to believe 意为使某人相信，to believe 是不定式作宾语补足语。
at all 加强否定语气，意为根本、完全。

sentences.sentence: When he reviewed original case studies of naturals, he found that exceptional memorizers were using techniques – sometimes without realizing it – and lots of practice.
sentences.translation: 当他重新审视关于天生记忆高手的原始案例研究时，他发现那些卓越的记忆者其实是在运用技巧——有时候自己都没意识到——再加上大量的练习。

structure.text: When he reviewed original case studies of naturals
that exceptional memorizers were using techniques

difficult_points: that 引导宾语从句，作 found 的宾语。
破折号中的 sometimes without realizing it 是插入语，说明这些记忆者有时并未意识到自己在用技巧。
and lots of practice 与 techniques 并列，都是 using 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: Often, exceptional memory was only for a single type of material, like digits.
sentences.translation: 通常，卓越的记忆力只针对单一类型的材料，比如数字。

structure.text: was only for a single type of material

difficult_points: like digits 举例说明单一类型材料具体指什么。
Often 置于句首作句子副词，表示这一现象是普遍规律而非个例。

sentences.sentence: "If we look at some of these memory tasks, they're the kind of thing most people don't even waste one hour practicing, but if they wasted 50 hours, they'd be exceptional at it," Ericsson says.
sentences.translation: 埃里克森说：“如果我们审视这些记忆任务中的一些，会发现它们是那种大多数人连一个小时都不愿花时间练习的事情，但如果他们花上50个小时去练习，就会在这方面变得非常出色。”

structure.text: "If we look at some of these memory tasks, they're the kind of thing most people don't even waste one hour practicing, but if they wasted 50 hours, they'd be exceptional at it,"
If we look at some of these memory tasks
most people don't even waste one hour practicing
but if they wasted 50 hours, they'd be exceptional at it

difficult_points: the kind of thing 后省略了关系代词 that，most people...practicing 是省略引导词的定语从句。
waste time doing 意为把时间浪费/花费在做某事上。
if they wasted...they'd be 是与现在事实相反的虚拟语气，表示假设。

sentences.sentence: It would be remarkable, he adds, to find a "person who is exceptional across a number of tasks.
sentences.translation: 他补充说，如果能找到一个在多项任务上都表现卓越的“人”，那将是非同寻常的。

structure.text: to find a "person who is exceptional across a number of tasks
who is exceptional across a number of tasks

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，真正的主语是后面的不定式 to find...。
he adds 是插入的说话动词短句，不影响主句结构。
引号在 person 前开启，但源文段落结尾处缺少对应的右引号，属于原始素材缺失，按原文保留。

expressions.text: it would be remarkable to do
expressions.meaning_zh: 做某事将是非同寻常的

sentences.sentence: I don't think that there's any compelling evidence that there are such people.
sentences.translation: 我不认为有任何令人信服的证据能证明存在这样的人。
difficult_points: don't think that 后接宾语从句，否定转移，实际语义是我认为没有证据。
第二个 that 引导同位语从句，说明 evidence 的具体内容。
